unresolved_risks: X-Forwarded-For is client-controlled — IP can be spoofed to bypass the limiter; acceptable for simple in-memory scope
_rate_limit_store dict keys are never pruned — memory grows with unique IPs; benign in normal operation
Rate-limited 429 responses bypass log_requests middleware (LIFO ordering) — 429s are not logged
rollback_command: git checkout main && git branch -D feat/build-ewa-rate-limiter-20260320
summary: Added sliding-window in-memory rate limiter middleware to backend/server.py. Each IP is limited to 60 requests per 60 seconds. Requests over the limit return HTTP 429 with JSON body {"error": "Rate limit exceeded", "status": 429}. IP extracted from X-Forwarded-For header or request.client.host. Three new pytest tests pass (429 after exceeding limit, 429 body shape, within-limit success). One pre-existing DR drill failure (test_intentional_failure) is unrelated to this change.
suggested_next: Merge feat/build-ewa-rate-limiter-20260320 to main via PR (Tier-2 approval). Future hardening: add per-minute log line for 429 responses, consider pruning stale IP keys from _rate_limit_store on a timer, or replace with Redis-backed limiter if multi-instance deploy is needed.
